codeReview.summary: Compared the prior failed rerun commit `af310fd` with the new fix commit `8d0d76e` against the round-7 scrutiny requirement. The new fix does address the specific collision from the prior review by registry-gating old sidecar-shaped payloads, and the public `InputItem` enum shape remains unchanged (`crates/agents-core/src/items.rs:10-12`). However, `prepare_input_with_session` still rewrites every empty `{}` into an in-band sidecar-bearing JSON object before invoking `session_input_callback` (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:39-58`), so the identity mechanism is not actually out-of-band and still occupies callback-visible JSON value space. Because `strip_empty_json_object_identity` only removes registered sidecars when the object is still a single-entry sentinel map (`crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:327-339`), any callback that mutates a seeded empty object in place can leak the internal sentinel key into prepared input and persisted session items. That violates the feature description and round-7 orchestrator note, so the fix does not adequately close the blocker.

issues.description: The empty-object identity path is still implemented as an in-band sidecar rewrite, not an out-of-band mechanism. `prepare_input_with_session` seeds history/new-turn empty `{}` values with sentinel JSON entries before calling `session_input_callback` (`session_persistence.rs:42-58`), and `seed_empty_json_object_identity` physically inserts those marker fields into the map (`session_persistence.rs:282-303`). That means callbacks observe rewritten JSON instead of the caller's original `{}` payloads. Worse, `strip_empty_json_object_identity` only strips the marker when the object is still the exact one-entry sentinel map (`session_persistence.rs:327-339`), so a callback that mutates a seeded empty object in place can leak the internal sentinel into prepared input and persisted session state. The prior collision bug is fixed, but the implementation still violates the required out-of-band, internal-only provenance contract and causes user-visible JSON rewriting for legitimate callback payloads.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission's shared docs do not record the session provenance invariant that `session_input_callback` inputs are public-facing `InputItem` values and provenance bookkeeping must stay completely out of user-visible JSON value space. That requirement currently exists only in repeated scrutiny notes / feature descriptions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json:5,789-797` repeats the out-of-band/user-visible constraint, but Grep found no `session_input_callback`, `empty-object`, or `provenance` guidance in mission `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, or `.factory/library/parity.md`.

summary: Fail: the rerun fixes the old sidecar-shape collision, but empty-object provenance is still carried by in-band sentinel JSON that rewrites callback-visible `{}` values and can leak into output when callbacks mutate those objects.
